  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The gap statement (free-form communication as the central limitation) and contribution claim (first typed inter-agent framework) are present but underspecified; 'improve' plausibly includes sharpening both the problem framing and the novelty claim's precision.",
    "R1.2": "The inferential chain from observed failure mode (hallucination propagation, ambiguous handoffs) to the proposed solution (schema-validated artifacts) has bridging steps that could be made more rigorous; cross-section coherence between the three prior-work threads and the limitation argument also warrants review.",
    "R1.4": "The 'first multi-agent LLM framework to enforce typed inter-agent communication' claim is a strong novelty assertion that requires scrutiny against the three cited threads and any omitted adjacent work (e.g., workflow orchestration systems, typed DSLs for agents).",
    "R1.6": "'Improve this draft' most directly signals prose and clarity work; sentence- and paragraph-level quality is a central expected responsibility regardless of broader interpretive scope."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should improvement be limited to writing quality (prose clarity, flow, sentence-level edits), or does it include substantive revision of argument structure, contribution framing, and novelty positioning?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The gap statement (free-form communication as the central limitation) and contribution claim (first typed inter-agent framework) are present but underspecified; 'improve' plausibly includes sharpening both the problem framing and the novelty claim's precision.
rationale.R1.2: The inferential chain from observed failure mode (hallucination propagation, ambiguous handoffs) to the proposed solution (schema-validated artifacts) has bridging steps that could be made more rigorous; cross-section coherence between the three prior-work threads and the limitation argument also warrants review.
rationale.R1.4: The 'first multi-agent LLM framework to enforce typed inter-agent communication' claim is a strong novelty assertion that requires scrutiny against the three cited threads and any omitted adjacent work (e.g., workflow orchestration systems, typed DSLs for agents).
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' most directly signals prose and clarity work; sentence- and paragraph-level quality is a central expected responsibility regardless of broader interpretive scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvement be limited to writing quality (prose clarity, flow, sentence-level edits), or does it include substantive revision of argument structure, contribution framing, and novelty positioning?
